title: Digimon Adventure: Bokura no War Game!
main_page_extract:
Yasuhiro Irie (入江 泰浩, Irie Yasuhiro; born March 30, 1971) is a Japanese animator, character designer and anime director.


== Biography ==
Beginning his career at Sunrise, Irie worked as an animation director and animator on several productions before transitioning into directing. He made his directorial debut with volumes 2–4 of Alien 9 and the 2004 television series Kurau Phantom Memory. He later directed Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood, a series noted for its storytelling and animation quality.
Irie's career is marked by versatility and experimentation with both traditional hand-drawn and digital animation techniques. His work on series such as Vision of Escaflowne, Cowboy Bebop, and RahXephon has contributed to his reputation as a dynamic storyteller. He is also a member of the Japan Animation Creators Association (JAniCA).


== Career and influence ==
Irie's influence extends beyond his directorial projects. His work on Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood has been described as a milestone in modern anime for its balance of character development, narrative pacing, and innovative animation techniques. Moreover, his incorporation of both traditional and digital methods has set new standards...
